questions.question: Part labelled A represents:
questions.explanation: Lysosomes are membrane-bound organelles containing digestive enzymes that break down waste materials and cellular debris.

questions.question: Part labelled B represents:
questions.explanation: The Golgi Apparatus modifies, sorts, and packages proteins and lipids for transport to other parts of the cell or for secretion.

questions.question: Part labelled C represents:
questions.explanation: The nucleolus is a dense region inside the nucleus where ribosomal RNA (rRNA) is synthesized and ribosome subunits are assembled.

questions.question: Part labelled D represents:
questions.explanation: The nucleus contains the cell's genetic material (DNA) and controls cellular activities such as growth, metabolism, and reproduction.

questions.question: Part labelled E represents:
questions.explanation: Rough Endoplasmic Reticulum (Rough ER) has ribosomes attached to its surface and is involved in protein synthesis and processing.

questions.question: Part labelled F represents:
questions.explanation: Smooth Endoplasmic Reticulum (Smooth ER) lacks ribosomes and is involved in lipid synthesis, detoxification, and calcium storage.

questions.question: Part labelled G represents:
questions.explanation: Ribosomes are small structures that synthesize proteins by translating messenger RNA. They can be free in the cytoplasm or attached to rough ER.

questions.question: Part labelled H represents:
questions.explanation: Centrioles are involved in cell division, organizing the microtubules of the spindle apparatus. They are found in animal cells but absent in most plant cells.

questions.question: Part labelled I represents:
questions.explanation: Mitochondria are the powerhouses of the cell, producing ATP through cellular respiration. They have their own DNA and double membranes.

questions.question: The image shown represents ____________
questions.explanation: The image shows a mitochondrion, characterized by its double membrane structure with inner membrane folded into cristae. Mitochondria are the powerhouses of the cell, producing ATP through cellular respiration.

questions.question: Part labelled J represents:
questions.explanation: The plasma membrane is a selectively permeable barrier that controls the movement of substances in and out of the cell.

questions.question: Part labelled K represents:
questions.explanation: Golgi vesicles transport modified proteins and lipids from the Golgi apparatus to other parts of the cell or to the plasma membrane for secretion.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You can identify animal cell organelles with precision.
scoreMessages.70: Good job. Review a few organelle structures and their functions.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on distinguishing between similar-looking organelles.
scoreMessages.0: Please review animal cell structure and organelle identification.
